documentation.readme: ROADtools (Rogue Office 365 and Azure (active) Directory tools)
usage_sections: Installation: There are multiple ways to install roadtx. Note that roadtx requires Python 3.10 or newer. Using a published version on PyPi Stable versions can be installed with pip install roadtx. This will automatically add the roadtx command to your PATH. Using a version from GitHub You can

documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [web, ad] in the recon category. Fits when the engagement needs: ROADtools (Rogue Office 365 and Azure (active) Directory tools).

documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: pip install roadlib/; pip install roadrecon/. Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `cd roadrecon/frontend/`. ROADtools (Rogue Office 365 and Azure (active) Directory tools).

name: ROADtools
summary: ROADtools (Rogue Office 365 and Azure (active) Directory tools)

trust.warning: Attribution / index entry only. The operator must audit the code, the README, the license, and the releases before invoking. Phase 5 — entry is auto-generated from the operator's curated fetch list; no automated enrichment yet.

use_cases: When you need recon, use ROADtools because ROADtools (Rogue Office 365 and Azure (active) Directory tools).
When you need a concrete invocation, run `cd roadrecon/frontend/` as documented upstream for ROADtools.
When chaining recon tools, pair ROADtools with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `ROADtools --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
